message:
fix(core): improve BOCPD numerics, lower regime detector thresholds, and refine AMAC executor

BOCPD change-point detector:
- Extract prior_predictive() so change-point hypothesis uses a broad
  uninformative prior (sigma=1000) per Adams & MacKay 2007 rather than
  reusing per-run predictive probabilities — fixes underweighting of
  change-point events for short run lengths
- Factor hazard_sum separately then multiply by prior_pred, avoiding
  double-counting per-run likelihood in the change-point slot
- Use iterators + zip instead of index-based loops for run-length
  suffix statistics to eliminate bounds-check overhead
- Replace inline 2.506... with const SQRT_2PI for clarity
- Use f64::mul_add for Welford M2 accumulation (fused multiply-add)
- Mark reset_cumsum as const fn

Regime shift detector:
- Lower CUSUM threshold 2.8 → 1.6 and posterior threshold 0.72 → 0.45
  to improve sensitivity to sustained contention shifts observed in
  production-like stress profiles
- Reformat long assert messages in tests for rustfmt compliance

AMAC executor:
- Merge Tool|Exec match arms sharing identical memory_weight (70)
- Use u64::abs_diff instead of manual if/else for variance computation
- Mark with_telemetry, telemetry, telemetry_mut as const fn
- Replace unchecked `as usize` cast with usize::try_from fallback
- Add tests: clone preserves/isolates telemetry state, config constructor
  matches parameters, default executor is enabled, batch telemetry
  round-trips through serde

Extensions (misc):
- Derive Default on HostcallSuperinstructionTelemetry instead of manual impl
- Replace is_multiple_of with modulo for recompile interval check
- Add has_ui field to RegistrySnapshot for stable UI availability queries
- Allow clippy::too_many_lines on pump_js_runtime_once
- Reformat dispatch_one call sites for rustfmt compliance

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
